---
title: "EnviBayes-INLA workshop"
subtitle: "22–24 June 2027 • Glasgow, UK"
title-block-banner: "#00000000"
title-block-banner-color: "white"
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
          #title-block-header {
            background-image: url(images/Glasgow_Cityscape.jpg);
            background-size: cover;
            background-position: center;
            height: 250px;
          }
          /* Title */
          .quarto-title-block .title {
            font-size: 3.5rem !important;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: white;
          }
          /* Subtitle */
          .quarto-title-block .subtitle {
            font-size: 1.6rem !important;
            color: white;
          }
          /* Metadata (if any) */
          #title-block-header.quarto-title-block.default .quarto-title-meta {
            color: white;
          }
          </style>

editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

<!-- ::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal" icon="false"} -->
<!-- **Abstract submission**  -->
<!-- now open - submit [here](https://github.com/EnviBayesINLA/workshop/docs)  -->
<!-- update link with google form -->

<!-- **Deadline**: XXXX -->
<!-- ::: -->


The EnviBayes–INLA Workshop 2027 is the first joint meeting between the EnviBayes and INLA workshop series, bringing together researchers developing Bayesian methods for environmental, ecological, spatial and spatio-temporal statistics. The programme will feature keynote lectures, contributed talks, poster sessions, and hands-on tutorials, covering a broad range of topics including INLA, spatial modelling, extreme value analysis, climate and weather, ecology, epidemiology, and environmental applications. The workshop builds on the success of previous EnviBayes workshops ([2023](https://statistics.colostate.edu/envibayes-workshop/?utm_source=chatgpt.com), [2025](https://sites.google.com/view/envibayes2025tamu/home?utm_source=chatgpt.com)) and the [INLA: Past, Present and Future workshop](https://inla-workshop.github.io/INLA-PastPresentFuture/), creating a unique opportunity for collaboration between these two vibrant research communities.







## Registration and Abstract Submission {#sec-regis}
::: coming-soon
Information about registration and abstract submission for talks and posters will be added here soon.
:::


## Important Dates {#sec-dates}

::: {.info-box}
::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### Contributed Talks & Posters Submission
- **Open**: Expected September 2026
- **Close**: Expected December 2026
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### Registration
- **Early Bird Opens**: Expected September 2026
- **Early Bird Closes**: Expected January 2027
- **Standard registration Closes**: Expected March 2027
:::
:::::
:::

## Registration Cost 

---

::: {.info-box}
::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### Non-Students
- **Early bird**:
- **Regular**:
- **Workshops**:
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### Students
- **Early bird**:
- **Regular**:
- **Workshops**:
:::
:::::
:::

## Keynote speakers {#sec-keynotes}

::: coming-soon
Information about Keynote speakers will be added here soon.
:::

<!-- ------------------------------------------------------------------------ -->

<!-- ::: {layout-ncol="3"} -->
<!-- ![Speaker1](images/Speaker1.png){width="185"} -->

<!-- ![Speaker2](images/Speaker1.png){width="185"} -->

<!-- ![Speaker3](images/Speaker1.png){width="185"} -->
<!-- ::: -->

## Contact {#sec-contact}

Questions about the conference? Join the discussion on [GitHub](https://github.com/EnviBayesINLA/workshop/discussions) or contact the main organisers [Daniela Castro-Camilo](mailto:daniela.castro-camilo@glasgow.ac.uk) and [Jafet Belmont Osuna](mailto:jafet.belmontosuna@glasgow.ac.uk).
